req.title: Audit log access
req.statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-014 defines two partitions: (1) privacy-officer querying their own organisation's audit logs — must succeed with scoped records; (2) privacy-officer querying a different organisation's audit logs — must be forbidden (403). A boundary case targets a single audit record that sits exactly on the organisation boundary (record belongs to org-A, queried by officer of org-A) to confirm the inclusion/exclusion logic at its tightest edge. EP covers the normal happy-path partition; NEG covers the cross-org forbidden path; BVA covers the single-record boundary edge. DT would further enumerate role×org combinations but the minimum required cases are addressed by these three.

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_logs
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists with role:privacy-officer scoped to org-A
At least one AuditEvent record exists for org-A
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer of org-A and obtain a bearer token
GET /AuditEvent with organisation filter set to org-A
expected: Response body is a valid FHIR Bundle
All returned AuditEvent resources have agent[0].who.reference scoped to org-A
No resources from any other organisation are present
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_privacy_officer_reads_own_org_audit_logs(
    fhir_base_url,
    officer_token_org_a,
    org_a_id,
    seed_audit_event_org_a,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    params = {"agent-organization": org_a_id, "_count": "50"}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        headers=headers,
        params=params,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "Expected at least one AuditEvent for org-A"
    for entry in entries:
        resource = entry["resource"]
        agents = resource.get("agent", [])
        org_refs = [
            a.get("who", {}).get("reference", "")
            for a in agents
        ]
        assert any(org_a_id in ref for ref in org_refs), (
            f"AuditEvent {resource.get('id')} not scoped to {org_a_id}"
        )

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_forbidden_cross_org_audit_logs
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists with role:privacy-officer scoped to org-A only
At least one AuditEvent record exists for org-B
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer of org-A and obtain a bearer token
GET /AuditEvent with organisation filter explicitly set to org-B
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome resource
No AuditEvent records from org-B are disclosed
trace: R-HLT-014: cross-organisation access shall be forbidden
R-HLT-014: error pathway — 403 when crossing org boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_privacy_officer_forbidden_cross_org_audit_logs(
    fhir_base_url,
    officer_token_org_a,
    org_b_id,
    seed_audit_event_org_b,
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    params = {"agent-organization": org_b_id}
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        headers=headers,
        params=params,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", (
        "Expected OperationOutcome on 403"
    )
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) >= 1
    assert issues[0].get("severity") == "error"

tests.name: test_privacy_officer_single_boundary_event_own_org
preconditions: A privacy-officer account exists with role:privacy-officer scoped to org-A
Exactly ONE AuditEvent record exists for org-A (minimum non-empty result set)
No AuditEvent records exist for org-A outside this single seed record
actions: Authenticate as privacy-officer of org-A and obtain a bearer token
GET /AuditEvent filtered to org-A expecting exactly one record
Verify the single record is included and no others leak through
expected: The single returned AuditEvent id matches the seeded record id
trace: R-HLT-014: records returned within org — boundary at count=1
R-HLT-014: BVA lower boundary of result set size
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_privacy_officer_single_boundary_event_own_org(
    fhir_base_url,
    officer_token_org_a,
    org_a_id,
    seed_single_audit_event_org_a,
):
    seeded_id = seed_single_audit_event_org_a  # fixture returns the AuditEvent id
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {officer_token_org_a}"}
    params = {
        "agent-organization": org_a_id,
        "_count": "10",
        "_sort": "-date",
    }
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        headers=headers,
        params=params,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, (
        f"Expected exactly 1 entry at lower boundary, got {len(entries)}"
    )
    returned_id = entries[0]["resource"]["id"]
    assert returned_id == seeded_id, (
        f"Returned id {returned_id} != seeded id {seeded_id}"
    )
    assert bundle.get("total") == 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_denied_audit_logs
preconditions: No valid authentication token is presented
actions: Send GET /AuditEvent with no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or WWW-Authenticate header
trace: R-HLT-014: role:privacy-officer precondition — absent role must not gain access
R-HLT-014: DT row — role=none, org=any => deny
